sections.title: A: Use the word given in capitals to form a word
sections.instructions: Use the word given in capitals at the end of each line to form a word that fits in the gap.

items.sentence: Although too many people in the West do still live in (1) ...... (POOR), on the whole, Westerners are far more (2) ...... (WEALTH) than those who live in developing countries.
Things that are essential to our (3) ...... (DAY) lives, and which we take for granted, such as electricity, are (4) ...... (LUXURY) to millions of people around the world.
Is this situation (5) ...... (ACCEPT) or should the West be giving more (6) ...... (ASSIST) to developing countries?
Some politicians and (7) ...... (ECONOMY) believe that Western financial (8) ...... (INVEST) in developing countries would be in everyone's interest.
As those countries get richer, they will become (9) ...... (VALUE) trading partners with the West.
The (10) ...... (REAL) is, however, that there is an enormous amount of work to be done before people in developing countries can enjoy the kind of lives that most people lead in the West.
Sharon came **D** into some money when her aunt died.
items.answer: D – into some money when her aunt died.

items.sentence: I manage to put **A** a little money by each month.
items.answer: A – a little money by each month.

items.sentence: You can bank **H** on house prices coming down soon.
items.answer: H – on house prices coming down soon.

items.sentence: I don't know how they can get **F** by on such a low salary.
items.answer: F – by on such a low salary.

items.sentence: If you could make **B** out the cheque to Peardrop Limited, that would be fine.
items.answer: B – out the cheque to Peardrop Limited, that would be fine.

items.sentence: The shop's giving **E** away free software with every computer.
items.answer: E – away free software with every computer.

items.sentence: I'm saving **C** up to buy a new DVD player.
items.answer: C – up to buy a new DVD player.

items.sentence: I don't know what we're going to live **G** on now I've lost my job.
items.answer: G – on now I've lost my job.

sections.title: C: Complete the second sentence using the word given
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words.

items.sentence:
I had to pay to park in the supermarket car park! **charged**
I ...... parking in the supermarket car park!

Is this credit card yours? **belong**
Does ...... you?

I haven't got much money in the bank. **amount**
I've only got ...... money in the bank.

Getting the car repaired was so expensive! **fortune**
It ...... to get the car repaired!

Sophie demanded that the shop assistant apologise to her. **apology**
Sophie demanded ...... the shop assistant.

Buying things online means I don't have to go into town so often. **saves**
Buying things online ...... to go into town so often.

Don't pay attention to what Dan says; your new shoes are lovely! **notice**
Take ...... what Dan says; your new shoes are lovely!

I haven't got much cash at the moment. **short**
I ...... cash at the moment.

Can you lend me some money? **borrow**
Can ......?
items.answer: I borrow some money from you

items.sentence: The shop assistant finally agreed ...... a full refund.
Bill asked me why ...... to go shopping with me.
Ellie asked Stan ...... to look at the new catalogue.
Stephen ...... me he'd bought that suit in a sale.
'What did the man say when you challenged him?' 'He said he ...... pay for the things in his bag, but I didn't believe him!'
When I last saw Carrie, she told me she ...... of buying a house, but now she's changed her mind.
'What did they say when they realised you weren't a shoplifter?' 'They apologised ...... me.'
This car is very ...... as it goes so far on one tank of petrol.
I can't believe how cheap these shorts were. They were a real ......!
What ...... of car does your mum drive?
Could you tell me who's in ...... here, please?
I paid the assistant and took the books but then stupidly left the bookshop without waiting for my .......
I think she ...... her fortune in the shipping industry.
I didn't find a new top but at ...... I got some new jeans.
